g.data-narration: FIFOs, also known as Named Pipes, are essential for interprocess communication in Unix systems.
A FIFO is a special filesystem object enabling unrelated processes to communicate using First-In-First-Out ordering.

text.tspan: FIFO (First In First Out) is a
special file in Unix that behaves
like a pipe - data flows sequentially.

g.data-narration: Key difference: Anonymous pipes need parent-child relationship, while FIFOs allow any unrelated processes to communicate.
FIFOs can be created using mkfifo command in shell or mkfifo() system call in C programs.
Opening a FIFO blocks until a process opens the other end. The O_NONBLOCK flag changes this default blocking behavior.
g.text: BOTH must be open for communication!

g.data-narration: FIFOs use standard file operations: read(), write(), and close() just like regular files after opening.

text.tspan: read() blocks until data available • write() blocks if buffer full • Use O_NONBLOCK to change

g.data-narration: FIFOs have a limited buffer (typically 64KB). When full, write() blocks or returns -1 with O_NONBLOCK set.
g.text: Data flows through buffer like a pipeline

g.data-narration: Use unlink() to remove a FIFO from the filesystem after use. This is essential for proper resource cleanup.
The producer-consumer example shows one process writing to a FIFO and another process reading from it.
Client-server uses two FIFOs: one for requests, one for responses. This enables bidirectional communication.
O_NONBLOCK flag prevents deadlocks by making operations return immediately instead of blocking.
g.text: Opens without blocking (even if no reader!)

g.data-narration: Two common FIFO errors: ENXIO when opening write end with no reader, and EACCES for permission denied.
g.text: Opening WR end with no reader

g.data-narration: Key exam points: FIFO vs anonymous pipe differences, blocking behavior, O_NONBLOCK flag, and buffer size limitations.
FIFOs are named pipes enabling unrelated processes to communicate with First-In-First-Out ordering using standard file operations.

text.tspan: FIFOs (Named Pipes) = Persistent filesystem objects for IPC between unrelated processes
Create: mkfifo() | Open: open() | Use: read()/write() | Remove: unlink()
Remember: Blocking behavior, O_NONBLOCK flag, 64KB buffer, two FIFOs for bidirectional
